title: Spacer
description: A spacer adds consistent vertical or horizontal space using UDS space tokens.
whenToUse: When CSS gap or margin is not available (e.g. in a flow where children cannot be flex/grid items). To enforce a specific token-sized gap.
whenNotToUse: When you can use CSS gap or margin with tokens directly. Prefer CSS layout properties.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders the correct gap for every size token (050–600)
Both vertical and horizontal axes apply the token to the correct dimension
Spacer takes no space when its size token resolves to 0
Spacer is not focusable and produces no SR output

props.name: data-axis
props.description: Whether the spacer adds vertical or horizontal whitespace

props.name: data-size
props.description: Spacing token to apply, mapping to --uds-space-{N}

states.name: not-applicable
states.description: Spacer is not focusable or interactive

dos: Prefer CSS gap or margin tokens directly when the parent is a flex/grid container
Use spacer only when the layout method does not allow gap (e.g. flow content)
donts: Don't put interactive content inside a spacer
Don't use spacer to push elements to the edges — use flex/grid alignment instead
Don't introduce custom sizes — pick from the documented spacing scale

keyboard.action: Not focusable; the spacer is decorative whitespace

screenReader.announcement: Spacer is invisible to assistive tech; meaning is conveyed by surrounding content order

_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Decorative only. Do not place interactive elements inside a spacer.
